target.title: fix(openrouter): apply strict9 tool_call_id sanitisation for Mistral routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Override `buildReplayPolicy` on the OpenRouter plugin so Mistral-targeted ids (`mistralai/...`, `openrouter/mistralai/...`, `mistral/...`) extend the existing passthrough policy with `sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: \"strict9\"` — the same contract the direct `mistral` provider already enforces.
- Out of scope: changing the shared `buildPassthroughGeminiSanitizingReplayPolicy`, touching non-Mistral OpenRouter routes (Gemini / OpenAI / Anthropic / DeepSeek keep their current passthrough policy), or altering Gemini thought-signature sanitisation.

## Linked context

Closes #58012

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. The issue is `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear` + `queueable-fix` + `source-repro`. Multiple earlier PRs (#58014, #58015, #57805, #47714) targeted the same gap and were closed without merging; ClawSweeper's most recent review encourages a fresh attempt against current main.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenRouter proxies Mistral but composed `PASSTHROUGH_GEMINI_REPLAY_HOOKS` straight through, so the replay sanitizer never converted tool_call_ids to Mistral's required 9-char format. Replayed assistant turns therefore failed with HTTP 400 `invalid_function_call` 3280 the moment the agent reused a stored tool_call_id.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source smoke against the registered OpenRouter plugin via `registerSingleProviderPlugin` and inspected the returned `ProviderReplayPolicy` for representative model ids, then re-ran the same smoke after `git stash`-ing this patch on the same branch.
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from the smoke.

  Before-fix (patch stashed):

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  mistralai/mistral-large-latest                strict? <none>    sanitizeToolCallIds: false
  openrouter/mistralai/mistral-small            strict? <none>    sanitizeToolCallIds: false
  google/gemini-2.5-pro                         strict? <none>    sanitizeToolCallIds: false
  bug repro: Mistral via OpenRouter has no strict9, will hit HTTP 400 invalid_function_call 3280 on tool replay
  ```

  After-fix (patch restored):

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  mistralai/mistral-large-latest                strict? strict9    sanitizeToolCallIds: true
  openrouter/mistralai/mistral-small            strict? strict9    sanitizeToolCallIds: true
  google/gemini-2.5-pro                         strict? <none>    sanitizeToolCallIds: false
  openai/gpt-5.4                                strict? <none>    sanitizeToolCallIds: false
  anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6                   strict? <none>    sanitizeToolCallIds: false
  ```

- Observed result after fix: OpenRouter-routed Mistral now matches the direct `mistral` provider on the replay sanitisation contract. Every other OpenRouter route keeps the existing passthrough behavior unchanged, and Gemini-backed routes still receive the `sanitizeThoughtSignatures` extension from the shared passthrough policy.
- What was not tested: A live OpenRouter call against `mistralai/mistral-large-latest` exercising the tool_call replay. The fix is at the replay policy boundary that produces the wire-level ids, so the smoke exercises the exact transformation that the live request would consume.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The smoke uses the real registered plugin in a local checkout but does not call OpenRouter because this environment is not configured with OpenRouter credentials. The reporter and the chain of closed PRs document the live 400 behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured above via `git stash`-ing this patch.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/openrouter/index.ts extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `extensions/openrouter/index.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 27 passed (27)` — the existing OpenRouter plugin coverage plus a new `OpenRouter Mistral tool_call_id strict9 (#58012)` describe block.
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added a parameterised `describe` covering: three Mistral id shapes get `strict9` (`mistralai/...`, `openrouter/mistralai/...`, `mistral/...`); four non-Mistral routes (Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek) keep the passthrough (no `strict9`); and Gemini routes still receive `sanitizeThoughtSignatures` from the shared passthrough policy.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix smoke above shows the new Mistral assertions had no `sanitizeToolCallIds`/`toolCallIdMode`, which is exactly the wire-level shape that produced the reported HTTP 400.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (positive). OpenRouter-routed Mistral conversations can now replay tool calls instead of failing with HTTP 400 `invalid_function_call` 3280.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No (tool_call_ids are rewritten in-flight only; nothing is persisted differently).

What is the highest-risk area?

A future Mistral-family id outside the `mistralai/...` / `openrouter/mistralai/...` / `mistral/...` prefix set would not pick up `strict9` automatically.

How is that risk mitigated?

The prefix list mirrors OpenRouter's existing Mistral routing conventions and is centralized in one helper (`isOpenRouterMistralModelId`) so any future route added to OpenRouter just needs an extra prefix here. Non-Mistral routes are explicitly verified by the parameterised passthrough test to ensure we did not over-broaden the strict9 contract.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on (a) the chosen prefix set and (b) whether the strict9 policy should live in `extensions/openrouter/` as it does here or be pushed into a shared helper alongside `buildPassthroughGeminiSanitizingReplayPolicy`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live OpenRouter call demonstrating the 400 → success transition is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet. The earlier closed PRs (#58014, #58015, #57805, #47714) are referenced for context.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, designed the override, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
Add an opt-in Telegram progress projection that renders already-authorized structured runtime events into one durable live message, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics or exposing new data by default.

## What this does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled, reasoning text and the existing structured runtime events are rendered into a **single durable live message** on the reasoning lane (Codex/Claude-CLI style), instead of the separate tool-progress lane.

It consumes the **existing** agent-event callbacks with **no contract change** — `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` — and falls back to the current `pushStreamToolProgress` lane whenever the flag is off.

## Scope boundary

Projection-only. It does **not** change prompts, model routing, tool execution, auth, or default visibility — it only changes Telegram rendering, and only when explicitly enabled.

- **Default off.** Gated on preview tool progress already being enabled **and** an explicit opt-in **and** a reasoning lane existing, so it inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate and cannot expose anything the tool-progress lane wasn't already authorized to show.
- **No raw data.** Tool lines show the tool name only (never raw args); item/plan/approval/command/patch lines show the title only (never raw command output); every line is whitespace-collapsed and length-capped.
- **No new event contract** (Pass 1). The shared structured-event contract already exists in `src/infra/agent-events.ts`; this PR only adds a Telegram renderer over it. Extracting/normalizing a shared contract is deliberately deferred to a follow-up once the UX is proven.

## Changes

- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`: add the opt-in `streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` flag.
- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts`: pure render helpers (gate, sanitize/cap, header-strip, message render, reasoning checkpoint, status-line append).
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`: wire the 7 callbacks through the renderer with fallback; clear the rolling timer in the run's `finally`.

## Tests

`extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts` (18 cases) covers:
- disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, and no-reasoning-lane (room-event / source-delivery-suppressed) all leave the gate off → current behaviour preserved, no progress leak.
- single `Thinking` header (no duplication); body stored header-stripped.
- rolling-timer suffix present while running, dropped (shorter strict-prefix) once cleared so a full-text lane update replaces stale timer text instead of appending.
- whitespace-collapse + length cap so no large/secret-looking value floods a line.
- reasoning checkpoint appends only newly-arrived text on cumulative re-delivery.

## Not yet covered

- End-to-end integration through the dispatch harness (timer-interval clearing on every exit path is wired in the run's `finally`; 429/backoff uses `stream.update` not forced flush — both verified by construction/reading, not yet by a harness test).
- Live current-head Telegram capture.

target.title: fix(gateway): include MCP tools in effective inventory
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Agent Tools dashboard / `tools.effective` inventory originally reported only static OpenClaw tools, so configured bundled MCP tools could be usable at runtime but absent from the session-effective inventory.
- Solution: split the Gateway surface so `tools.effective` is a read-only peek projection with explicit MCP notices, and add `tools.effective.refresh` as an `operator.write` method that performs live MCP discovery/materialization on demand.
- What changed: effective inventory projection supports `source: "mcp"`; session MCP runtimes expose peek-only catalog metadata; the Gateway read path no longer cold-starts MCP transports; the refresh path materializes MCP tools through the existing runtime/materializer/final-policy path; Control UI adds an explicit **Refresh Available Tools** action; SDK/docs/protocol/tests cover the new split.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider transport changes, no Tool Search/Code Mode raw provider-body changes, no auto-reply `/tools` command changes, no install-package-dir changes, no broad shared-runtime refactor, and no claim that #80909 or the reporter-specific SSE raw provider-body path is fixed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A (intentionally not closing #76724; this keeps the reporter-specific SSE/runtime-provider-body path out of scope)
- Related / Refs #76724
- Related #70876
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

#76724 reports MCP handshake success while the Agent Tools dashboard stays stuck at the pre-MCP tool count and does not list MCP tools. Runtime code can materialize bundled MCP tools for model runs, but the dashboard inventory used a separate static inventory path. The initial patch made `tools.effective` perform live MCP materialization; after review of the read/security boundary, this update keeps `tools.effective` read-only and moves live discovery behind an explicit refresh method.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.effective` should not cold-start/list MCP transports from a read-scoped dashboard load, while the dashboard must still have an explicit path to discover configured MCP tools and then show the warm catalog.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this branch at `3c7a4e67b6`, QA mock OpenAI provider, real stdio MCP server created by the proof harness, Tool Search disabled, Code Mode disabled.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT=/root/repos/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective \
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAD=3c7a4e67b6 \
OPENCLAW_UPSTREAM_COMMIT=110042d840 \
OPENCLAW_RPC_PROOF_SUMMARY=/tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof-summary.json \
node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-mcp-tools-effective-d-proof.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Copied live Gateway RPC harness output from this branch:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "D read/refresh split: cold read peek-only, explicit refresh materializes MCP, warm read peeks catalog",
  "upstreamCommit": "110042d840",
  "localHead": "3c7a4e67b6",
  "cold": {
    "noticeIds": ["mcp-not-yet-connected"],
    "containsMcpTarget": false,
    "runtimeCreated": false,
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 }
    ]
  },
  "refreshed": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "runtimeCreated": true,
    "catalogToolNames": ["probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  },
  "warm": {
    "noticeIds": [],
    "containsMcpTarget": true,
    "matchingIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"],
    "groupSummaries": [
      { "id": "core", "source": "core", "toolCount": 18 },
      { "id": "plugin", "source": "plugin", "toolCount": 2 },
      { "id": "mcp", "source": "mcp", "toolCount": 1, "sampleIds": ["reproProbe__probe_tool"] }
    ]
  }
}
```

Additional Control UI browser proof from current head `b8bcb372b3d08b2b193d894b67d486cd9fc376fc`:

```text
Cold Control UI load:
- Panel shows: "MCP servers are configured but not connected yet. Use Refresh Available Tools to discover configured MCP tools."
- `Probe Tool` is absent from Available Right Now.
- Live count is 0.

After clicking "Refresh Available Tools":
- Browser RPC request observed: tools.effective.refresh with {agentId: "main", sessionKey: "agent:main:main"}.
- Available Right Now lists `Probe Tool` with source `MCP`.
- Live count is 1.
```

Screenshots are hosted on a separate fork artifact branch, not committed to this PR diff.

<details open>
<summary>Cold read: configured-MCP notice visible; Probe Tool absent</summary>

![Cold Control UI MCP notice](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nxmxbbd/openclaw/pr-84096-ui-proof/01-cold-mcp-notice.png)
</details>

<details open>
<summary>After refresh: Probe Tool appears as an MCP tool</summary>

![After refresh MCP tool](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nxmxbbd/openclaw/pr-84096-ui-proof/02-after-refresh-mcp-tool.png)
</details>

- Observed result after fix: a cold `tools.effective` read returns only core/plugin groups plus `mcp-not-yet-connected` and does not create a session MCP runtime; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes the configured stdio MCP server and returns `reproProbe__probe_tool`; the next `tools.effective` read shows the warm MCP group via peek without another cold materialization.
- What was not tested: the reporter's original Flask SSE server and release version `v2026.5.2`; Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior; #80909; full cross-platform/browser E2E. This PR is scoped to the Gateway/dashboard effective-inventory boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-patch proof on current main returned 20 tools and omitted `reproProbe__probe_tool` from `tools.effective` while the runtime provider path could include it.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `tools.effective` called the static `resolveEffectiveToolInventory()` resolver, which builds from `createOpenClawCodingTools()` only. Bundled MCP tools are appended later in the model-run path through the session MCP runtime/materializer, so the dashboard inventory and runtime provider path diverged.
- Boundary correction in this update: live MCP discovery/listing can start configured stdio/remote transports, so it now lives behind explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`; the read-scoped method only peeks at an existing matching-fingerprint runtime catalog and otherwise returns a notice.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered core/plugin/channel grouping but did not cover bundled MCP catalog projection, cold-read side effects, refresh materialization, or stale MCP config fingerprints through the `tools.effective` handler/RPC path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end-style Gateway RPC proof
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts`
  - `packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts`
  - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: cold `tools.effective` does not create/list MCP and returns notices; warm read projects an existing same-fingerprint catalog; fingerprint mismatch returns refresh-needed notice instead of stale MCP tools; explicit `tools.effective.refresh` materializes MCP tools and applies final effective policy; UI uses read on normal load and refresh on explicit action.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug and security-boundary concern live at the seam between static inventory, session MCP runtime, Gateway method scope, SDK, and UI refresh behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none found for bundled MCP in `tools-effective` / `tools-effective-inventory` before this patch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Agent Tools panel now loads without implicitly starting configured MCP transports. If MCP servers are configured but not connected/listed yet, the panel shows an explanatory notice and a **Refresh Available Tools** action. Triggering refresh performs live discovery and lets subsequent reads show the warm MCP tools.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before this PR:
[tools.effective] -> [static createOpenClawCodingTools inventory] -> [core/plugin/channel only]

Updated design:
[tools.effective]         -> [static inventory + peek existing MCP catalog] -> [core/plugin/channel + notices or warm mcp]
[tools.effective.refresh] -> [session MCP runtime materialize] -> [final bundled-tool policy] -> [core/plugin/channel + mcp]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No new capability beyond existing configured MCP materialization; yes, a new explicit Gateway method is added.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes, only from explicit `tools.effective.refresh` or existing run-lifecycle MCP materialization, not from cold `tools.effective` reads.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, refresh can start/list configured MCP transports; it does not invoke MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: live MCP listing can start a configured stdio server or connect to a configured remote endpoint, so this update moves that side effect out of `operator.read` and into explicit `operator.write` `tools.effective.refresh`. The read path only peeks an existing runtime catalog with a matching config fingerprint and returns notices otherwise. Refresh reuses the existing session MCP runtime/materializer/final effective policy path and releases the materializer lease.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local dev environment
- Runtime/container: local Node via `node --import tsx`
- Model/provider: QA mock OpenAI provider, `mock-openai/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway WebSocket RPC `sessions.create`, `tools.effective`, `tools.effective.refresh`, then `tools.effective`
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.profile: "coding"`, `tools.toolSearch: false`, `tools.codeMode.enabled: false`, `mcp.servers.reproProbe` stdio server command `node <tmp>/dummy-mcp-server.mjs`

### Steps

1. Start QA mock provider and local Gateway from this branch.
2. Create a session with `sessions.create`.
3. Call cold `tools.effective` and assert no MCP runtime was created and `reproProbe__probe_tool` is absent.
4. Call `tools.effective.refresh` and assert `reproProbe__probe_tool` appears in an `mcp` source group.
5. Call warm `tools.effective` and assert the MCP group is projected via peek.

### Expected

- Cold read: no MCP cold-start/listing; notice returned.
- Refresh: MCP listed/materialized through explicit write-scoped method.
- Warm read: MCP group visible through existing catalog peek.

### Actual

- Cold read: `runtimeCreated: false`, `containsMcpTarget: false`, `noticeIds: ["mcp-not-yet-connected"]`.
- Refresh: `runtimeCreated: true`, `matchingIds: ["reproProbe__probe_tool"]`, `catalogToolNames: ["probe_tool"]`.
- Warm read: `containsMcpTarget: true`, no stale not-connected notice.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD` → exit 0
  - `git diff --exit-code $(git merge-base upstream/main HEAD)..HEAD -- CHANGELOG.md` → no contributor-authored changelog entry remains
  - `pnpm check:test-types` → passed
  - `pnpm lint --threads=8` → passed
  - `pnpm protocol:check` → passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/tools-effective.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.request-boundary.test.ts packages/sdk/src/index.test.ts` → 9 files / 110 tests passed
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts packages/sdk/src/index.e2e.test.ts` → 1 file passed; 4 passed / 1 skipped
  - `cd ui && pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts src/ui/views/agents-panels-tools-skills.browser.test.ts src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` → 3 files / 28 tests passed
  - real Gateway RPC proof with real stdio MCP server verifies cold read / refresh / warm read behavior
  - local Control UI browser proof at head `b8bcb372b3d08b2b193d894b67d486cd9fc376fc` verifies cold configured-MCP notice/no `Probe Tool`, explicit `tools.effective.refresh`, then `Probe Tool` visible as an MCP tool
- Edge cases checked:
  - final effective policy still filters materialized MCP tools
  - stale MCP config fingerprints produce refresh-needed behavior instead of serving stale MCP tools
  - read path does not call `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime()` on cold load
  - UI notice rendering and explicit refresh action
  - SDK helper calls `tools.effective.refresh`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - reporter's exact Flask SSE MCP server / v2026.5.2 setup
  - Tool Search / Code Mode raw provider-body behavior (out of scope)
  - #80909 raw provider-body behavior (out of scope)
  - full cross-platform/browser E2E

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: users may expect MCP tools to appear immediately on panel load.
  - Mitigation: the panel now displays MCP notices and an explicit refresh action; warm catalogs still appear in read-only inventory.
- Risk: refresh can start configured MCP transports.
  - Mitigation: refresh is explicit and registered as `operator.write`; cold `operator.read` inventory no longer starts or lists MCP transports.
- Risk: dashboard/policy projection may still differ from a live inbound-message run when the UI lacks sender identity.
  - Mitigation: do not accept caller-supplied auth/delivery context; use server-derived session fields and final policy only.
- Risk: overlap with #70876.
  - Mitigation: this PR is narrower and avoids unrelated auto-reply/install-package-dir changes; related work is explicitly linked.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
Add an opt-in Telegram progress projection that renders already-authorized structured runtime events into one durable live message, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics or exposing new data by default.

## What this does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled, reasoning text and the existing structured runtime events are rendered into a **single durable live message** on the reasoning lane (Codex/Claude-CLI style), instead of the separate tool-progress lane.

It consumes the **existing** agent-event callbacks with **no contract change** — `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` — and falls back to the current `pushStreamToolProgress` lane whenever the flag is off.

## Scope boundary

Projection-only. It does **not** change prompts, model routing, tool execution, auth, or default visibility — it only changes Telegram rendering, and only when explicitly enabled.

- **Default off.** Gated on preview tool progress already being enabled **and** an explicit opt-in **and** a reasoning lane existing, so it inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate and cannot expose anything the tool-progress lane wasn't already authorized to show.
- **No raw data.** Tool lines show the tool name only (never raw args); item/plan/approval/command/patch lines show the title only (never raw command output); every line is whitespace-collapsed and length-capped.
- **No new event contract** (Pass 1). The shared structured-event contract already exists in `src/infra/agent-events.ts`; this PR only adds a Telegram renderer over it. Extracting/normalizing a shared contract is deliberately deferred to a follow-up once the UX is proven.

## Changes

- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`: add the opt-in `streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` flag.
- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts`: pure render helpers (gate, sanitize/cap, header-strip, message render, reasoning checkpoint, status-line append).
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts`: wire the 7 callbacks through the renderer with fallback; clear the rolling timer in the run's `finally`.

## Tests

`extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts` (18 cases) covers:
- disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, and no-reasoning-lane (room-event / source-delivery-suppressed) all leave the gate off → current behaviour preserved, no progress leak.
- single `Thinking` header (no duplication); body stored header-stripped.
- rolling-timer suffix present while running, dropped (shorter strict-prefix) once cleared so a full-text lane update replaces stale timer text instead of appending.
- whitespace-collapse + length cap so no large/secret-looking value floods a line.
- reasoning checkpoint appends only newly-arrived text on cumulative re-delivery.

## Not yet covered

- End-to-end integration through the dispatch harness (timer-interval clearing on every exit path is wired in the run's `finally`; 429/backoff uses `stream.update` not forced flush — both verified by construction/reading, not yet by a harness test).
- Live current-head Telegram capture.

target.title: Recover message-tool-only private final replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Recover `message_tool_only` turns where the assistant wrote the source reply only as private final text.
- Retry once with an explicit `message(action="send")` steer when the current source reply is missing.
- If the retry still does not produce current-source delivery evidence, return a visible runtime error payload instead of leaving the source chat silent.
- Preserve existing planning-only retry behavior and deliberate `NO_REPLY` silence.

## Problem

In `message_tool_only` delivery mode, private final text is not delivered to the source conversation. Before this change, an agent could produce the user-facing answer as private final text and the source channel would appear stuck.

The fix must not treat unrelated message sends as success. A message sent to another explicit target or session does not prove the current source conversation was answered.

## Fix

- Add a `message_tool_only` missing-source-reply recovery path in `runEmbeddedAgent`.
- Require current-source reply evidence via `messagingToolSourceReplyPayloads` before suppressing recovery.
- Add one replay-safe retry with `MESSAGE_TOOL_ONLY_RETRY_INSTRUCTION`.
- Add a source-suppressed visible error payload when retry is unavailable or exhausted.
- Clarify the system prompt so final-only answers are explicitly described as private in `message_tool_only` mode.

## Guardrails

- Explicit-route or cross-session `message` sends do not count as current-source replies.
- Planning-only turns still take the existing planning retry path before message-tool-only recovery.
- `NO_REPLY` remains silent and does not trigger retry/error recovery.
- Private final text is not republished as a source reply; the agent must use the message tool.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A `message_tool_only` source chat must not stay silent when the assistant only writes private final text. The runtime retries once with a message-tool delivery instruction and then surfaces a visible error if the source reply still was not sent.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Ubuntu, using the repo's actual Node/Corepack/pnpm validation path against the runner implementation and prompt snapshot contract.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ corepack pnpm format:check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ corepack pnpm test:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 196 passed (196)

$ corepack pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
$ node scripts/check-changed.mjs -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] typecheck core
[check:changed] typecheck core tests
[check:changed] lint core
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
(exit 0)
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output above from the patched worktree. The regression tests exercise the source-channel failure mode directly: private-final-only retries, exhausted retry returns visible error, explicit-route sends do not count as source replies, planning-only retry wins first, and `NO_REPLY` stays silent.

**Observed result after fix:** The patched runner no longer treats broad message-tool delivery evidence as proof of source delivery, preserves existing silent/planning paths, and passes the targeted runner tests plus full changed-file validation.

**What was not tested:** Native Telegram Desktop visual proof was not captured in this PR body. The repository's Mantis Telegram proof job attempted that path separately but failed before Desktop start because the Crabbox/AWS capture environment could not launch, not because this patch's runner checks failed.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

## Replaces

Replaces #87455 with a smaller branch focused on the original runtime failure, while keeping the relevant guard coverage that ClawSweeper flagged on the first version of this replacement PR.

target.title: fix(memory-core): show signalCount in promotion annotations
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87588 — Dreaming promotion annotation shows `recallCount` instead of `signalCount`, causing confusion when entries are promoted via `dailyCount` signals with `recallCount=0`.

## Root Cause

The promotion annotation format in `buildPromotionSection()` was showing only `candidate.recallCount`:

```
[score=0.832 recalls=0 avg=0.620 source=memory/2026-05-24-0003.md:9-10]
```

But the actual promotion gate checks `signalCount = recallCount + dailyCount + groundedCount`. This meant entries could show `recalls=0` even though they passed the `minRecallCount` gate via `dailyCount` signals, confusing users auditing promotion decisions.

## Fix

Changed the annotation format to show both `signals` (total) and `recalls` (recall count only):

```
[score=0.832 signals=7 recalls=0 avg=0.620 source=memory/2026-05-24-0003.md:9-10]
```

**Files changed:**
- `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts:1677` — Show `signals=${signalCount} recalls=${recallCount}` in annotations
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:600,627` — Include signalCount in verbose logging
- `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts:1405` — Show signals count in CLI output
- `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — Added regression test verifying annotation shows `signals=7` when `recallCount=0, dailyCount=6, groundedCount=1`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Promotion annotations were showing `recalls=0` for entries promoted via `dailyCount` signals, confusing users about why entries were promoted. Analysis of 79 promoted entries showed 58 had `recallCount=0` but passed via `dailyCount=5-7`.

- **Real environment tested:** Worktree at `/home/0668000844/_workspace/openclaw` with Node.js v20.20.2, Bun v1.2.12, testing the memory-core extension promotion annotation output.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # 1. Created test workspace and seeded memory entry
  # 2. Modified short-term recall store to set recallCount=0, dailyCount=6, groundedCount=1
  # 3. Ran rankShortTermPromotionCandidates() to get candidates
  # 4. Ran applyShortTermPromotions() to generate MEMORY.md
  # 5. Verified annotation format in generated MEMORY.md
  
  # Regression test command (requires built project):
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts --test-name-pattern "shows signalCount"
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  ✓ short-term-promotion.test.ts - shows signalCount instead of just recallCount in promotion annotations
  
  Generated MEMORY.md annotation:
  <!-- openclaw-memory-promotion:test-key-xyz -->
  - Entry with dailyCount signals but zero recallCount. [score=0.850 signals=7 recalls=0 avg=0.850 source=memory/2026-05-28.md:5-6]
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** 
  - Annotation now shows `signals=7` (total signal count) before `recalls=0`
  - Users can now see the entry was promoted via total signals (0+6+1=7), not just recalls
  - The `recalls=0` field is still shown for audit purposes
  - Test verifies: `expect(memoryText).toContain("signals=7")` and `expect(memoryText).toContain("recalls=0")`

- **What was not tested:** Did not run a full live memory-core dreaming session with real user data and months of memory files. The fix is a pure display format change verified through the full `applyShortTermPromotions()` integration test path with controlled test data.

## Test plan

- Coverage level: Integration test
- Target test file: `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`
- Scenario: Entry with `recallCount=0, dailyCount=6, groundedCount=1` produces annotation with `signals=7 recalls=0`
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises the annotation format generation path, catching any future regression where signalCount is not calculated or displayed correctly.

## Note on parameter naming

The `minRecallCount` configuration parameter name is intentionally kept unchanged for backwards compatibility, even though it actually gates on `signalCount`. Renaming it would break existing user configurations. The annotation fix makes the actual behavior clear to users.

target.title: fix(native-hook-relay): prune stale bridge files on registration
target.body:
> **AI-assisted PR** — diagnostic instrumentation, repro analysis, and patch authoring were done in a co-working session with Claude (Anthropic) on top of Cowork, and with ARC (a local OpenClaw agent on my install) as a second pair of eyes. All real-behavior tests below were run by me against my own gateway. I understand what the code does and have reviewed every line of the diff manually.

## Summary

Fixes the root cause behind issues #87536 and #73723: every native tool call in a Codex-backed session can return `Native hook relay unavailable` because Codex picks a stale bridge registration file from a dead prior gateway process.

After a crash, `SIGUSR1` supervisor-restart, or any non-graceful shutdown, the file in `/tmp/openclaw-native-hook-relays-<uid>/<hash>.json` is left pointing to a dead PID and an unbound port. The current gateway's bridge then competes with these zombies in the directory, and Codex clients can pick a stale entry. Once a client locks onto a stale entry, every hook invocation for that session fails until the next clean restart.

## Fix

In `registerNativeHookRelayBridge`, before doing anything else, scan the bridge directory and remove any `.json` record whose owning PID is **dead** (or whose `expiresAtMs` has passed). Records owned by other **live** same-uid OpenClaw processes (e.g., a parallel `openclaw-beacon-gateway.service`) are preserved.

Total: +49 / −1 lines in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`.

```ts
function registerNativeHookRelayBridge(registration: ActiveNativeHookRelayRegistration): void {
  // Prune actually stale bridge files from prior gateway processes. The bridge
  // directory is scoped by OS user (uid) and is shared across all OpenClaw
  // gateways/profiles run by that user, so a record with a non-current PID is
  // NOT automatically stale — it can legitimately belong to another live
  // gateway under the same uid. Only prune records whose owning PID is dead
  // or whose expiry has passed; leave live foreign records alone.
  try {
    const staleDir = ensureNativeHookRelayBridgeDir();
    const now = Date.now();
    for (const name of readdirSync(staleDir)) {
      if (!name.endsWith(".json")) { continue; }
      const full = path.join(staleDir, name);
      try {
        const rec = JSON.parse(readFileSync(full, "utf8")) as {
          pid?: number; expiresAtMs?: number;
        };
        if (!rec || typeof rec.pid !== "number" || rec.pid === process.pid) {
          continue;
        }
        let pidAlive = false;
        try { process.kill(rec.pid, 0); pidAlive = true; } catch { pidAlive = false; }
        const expired = typeof rec.expiresAtMs === "number" && now > rec.expiresAtMs;
        if (pidAlive && !expired) {
          // Live foreign record from another same-uid gateway/profile. Preserve it.
          continue;
        }
        rmSync(full, { force: true });
        log.debug("pruned stale native hook relay bridge file", {
          file: name, stalePid: rec.pid, currentPid: process.pid,
          reason: !pidAlive ? "dead-pid" : "expired",
        });
      } catch { /* ignore unparseable / racing files */ }
    }
  } catch (error) {
    log.debug("native hook relay bridge dir prune skipped", { error });
  }
  // ... rest of function unchanged ...
}
```

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

Every native tool call in a Codex-backed session returns `Native hook relay unavailable` whenever the bridge directory `/tmp/openclaw-native-hook-relays-<uid>/` contains stale `.json` records pointing to dead PIDs from prior gateway processes (after crashes, `SIGUSR1` supervisor-restarts, or non-graceful shutdowns). Codex clients pick a stale entry, the bridge HTTP server they try to reach is gone, and every PreToolUse hook invocation is blocked. This blocks `date`, `time date`, file reads, MCP memory queries, and any other native tool from agent turns until the operator manually cleans the directory and restarts the gateway. Same underlying mechanism as #73723. The fix also has to **avoid breaking the multi-gateway case** where the same uid runs more than one OpenClaw profile (caught in initial review): the new predicate preserves bridge records owned by other live same-uid processes and only removes records whose PID is dead or whose `expiresAtMs` has passed.

### Real setup tested

- OpenClaw `2026.5.26` running as a systemd user service (`openclaw-gateway.service`)
- Ubuntu 22.04 in WSL2 on Windows 11
- Node 24.15.0 (bundled with the gateway runtime)
- Codex plugin enabled with: `transport: stdio`, `mode: yolo`, `approvalPolicy: never`, `sandbox: danger-full-access`
- A real ARC-named agent operating over the Discord channel surface, with real Codex subagent tool invocations
- Live parallel `openclaw-beacon-gateway.service` running under the **same uid (1000)** on the same machine, sharing `/tmp/openclaw-native-hook-relays-1000/`. This is the exact "same-uid multi-gateway" topology the review flagged.

### Exact steps or command run after the patch

1. Applied the equivalent of this PR's source diff to the bundled `dist/native-hook-relay-AN6S_wz5.js` on my live install.
2. Restarted only `openclaw-gateway.service` via `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway.service`. Left `openclaw-beacon-gateway.service` running.
3. Confirmed `openclaw-beacon-gateway.service` still answers requests after the ARC gateway restart (its bridge record was preserved, not pruned).
4. From ARC over the Discord channel: ran `date`, then ran `date` again, then ran `time date`, with variable gaps (immediate, ~30 seconds, ~3 minutes) across multiple Codex subagent sessions.
5. Inspected `/tmp/openclaw-native-hook-relays-1000/` and `ss -ltn` before and after restart to confirm the zombie cleanup and live-record preservation.

### After-fix evidence

**1. Bridge directory snapshot — before patch (5 zombies + 1 live ARC + 1 live Beacon):**

```
$ ls -la /tmp/openclaw-native-hook-relays-1000/
-rw-------  pid=11601  port=37109   # dead from prior session
-rw-------  pid=17733  port=46593   # dead
-rw-------  pid=90952  port=38385   # dead
-rw-------  pid=211925 port=44387   # dead
-rw-------  pid=240009 port=43303   # dead
-rw-------  pid=243162 port=46523   # <-- live ARC gateway
-rw-------  pid=229591 port=18889   # <-- live Beacon gateway (different profile, same uid)
```

**2. Before patch, every native tool call from ARC was blocked (live terminal output via Discord):**

```
> run date
Command blocked by PreToolUse hook: Native hook relay unavailable.
Command: date

> run time date
Command blocked by PreToolUse hook: Native hook relay unavailable.
Command: time date
```

**3. After patch — prune output on first registration after ARC gateway restart (copied live from runtime log at `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-28.log`):**

```
pruned stale native hook relay bridge file file=084c22487544d61d08bb88f20d738f6b.json stalePid=240009 currentPid=243162 reason=dead-pid
pruned stale native hook relay bridge file file=2413c88dfc4ad1e4e37edfe66958a932.json stalePid=211925 currentPid=243162 reason=dead-pid
pruned stale native hook relay bridge file file=90c38fd2c272f3d6c9d03a346dce35f3.json stalePid=90952 currentPid=243162 reason=dead-pid
pruned stale native hook relay bridge file file=654149472e7cd9034900a8d2dd43661e.json stalePid=17733 currentPid=243162 reason=dead-pid
pruned stale native hook relay bridge file file=5835f8d6faece4fb32fcec4784cd25d3.json stalePid=11601 currentPid=243162 reason=dead-pid
# Note: Beacon's bridge record (pid 229591) was NOT pruned — process.kill(229591, 0) succeeded, so the record was preserved.
```

**4. After patch — native tool calls succeed across multiple turns and time intervals (copied live from Discord transcript):**

```
> run date
Thu May 28 02:55:15 EDT 2026

> run date
Thu May 28 02:55:19 EDT 2026

> run date
Thu May 28 03:18:41 EDT 2026

> run time date
Thu May 28 03:21:04 EDT 2026
real    0m0.001s
user    0m0.000s
sys     0m0.001s
```

Five consecutive native calls in one shell process passed. Separate Codex tool invocations across multiple runs also passed. `time date` composite commands passed. File reads and AgentMemory MCP queries from agent turns also passed.

**5. After patch — bridge directory state, dead corpses pruned + both live processes preserved:**

```
$ ls -la /tmp/openclaw-native-hook-relays-1000/
-rw-------  pid=243162 port=46523   # ARC gateway (current process that just registered)
-rw-------  pid=229591 port=18889   # Beacon gateway (live foreign record, preserved)
```

Beacon kept answering tool calls in `working-room` (the auto-joined Discord voice channel) without re-registration, confirming the live-foreign-record preservation works end-to-end.

### Observed result

Before this fix the failure was 100% reproducible after a `SIGUSR1` supervisor-restart or non-graceful gateway exit had left even one zombie record behind. After this fix the same test cycle shows zero `Native hook relay unavailable` responses across all native tool invocations, **and** the parallel Beacon gateway continues operating without interruption (its bridge record is not pruned by the ARC gateway's startup scan).

### Not tested

- I did not test on macOS or native Linux (only Ubuntu 22.04 in WSL2 with Node 24.15.0).
- I did not test the PID-recycling edge case where a previously-OpenClaw PID has been recycled by a non-OpenClaw process between two registrations; the predicate currently falls toward "preserve" in that case, and the record is cleaned the next time the recycled PID either dies or the `expiresAtMs` passes. This is the safe direction (false-preserve over false-delete).
- I did not add an in-repo unit test that asserts "live foreign record preserved, dead-PID record pruned" — the existing `native-hook-relay.test.ts` doesn't have a fixture for spawning a real foreign-PID record, and adding one expands the PR scope. Happy to follow up if maintainers prefer.
- I did not run the repo's full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` matrix from the fork (30+ minute pipeline locally; CI will exercise it).

## Why this is safe

- **No public API change.** The function signature is unchanged; behavior is purely additive.
- **No effect on the happy path.** When the bridge dir is already clean, the loop is a no-op (~ms cost).
- **Live foreign records are preserved.** Same-uid multi-profile setups continue to work.
- **`process.kill(pid, 0)` is signal-free under POSIX** — it is a permission/existence probe only, it never interrupts the foreign process.
- **Errors are swallowed.** A racing read/delete or unparseable file just gets skipped — the registration of the current bridge proceeds regardless.
- **Idempotent.** Running registration multiple times produces the same final state.

## What this PR does NOT do

This PR is intentionally scoped to the defensive fix. The broader architectural question of whether `renderNativeHookRelayUnavailableResponse` should fail-open instead of fail-closed (suggested in #73723 and discussed in #87536) is a separate decision about safety contract and should land in its own PR with an explicit config knob and a maintainer discussion of the default.

I also have local-only patches that defer the dedup file delete by 200ms and suppress the timer-fired `handle.unregister` from the Codex plugin's run-attempt module; both are useful as defense in depth but are not strictly necessary once this prune lands, so they are not included here.

## Refs

- Closes the recurring symptom in #87536
- Addresses the same underlying cause as #73723
- Incorporates ClawSweeper review feedback on same-uid multi-gateway preservation (#87563 review comment)
- I checked `release/2026.5.27` (source of `2026.5.27-beta.1`) — this fix is complementary, not duplicative; happy to retarget the PR there if maintainers prefer

— Adam Houk, Applied AI Solutions
target.author: applied-ai-solutions-hub

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N x 3 x chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix a TS error in `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: add missing `getSession` to subagent mock.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits, the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks.

Real environment tested: Vitest test run on Linux Node 22 (local checkout).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

Evidence after fix:
The circuit breaker tests verify:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

Fix applied: partialSummaryFallback now returns isGenericFallback=false (contains useful progress from earlier chunks), only truly generic fallback (no partial summary) returns isGenericFallback=true.

Observed result after fix: Partial summaries preserve useful session context while still stopping repeated calls on persistent generic failures.

What was not tested: Live compaction with real unavailable summarizer model.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: FallbackSummaryResult type, circuit breaker in summarizeInStages, partialSummaryFallback classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

Fixes #58838

target.title: fix(memory): show signalCount in dreaming promotion annotation (#87588)
target.body:
## Problem

The promotion annotation written to `MEMORY.md` has shown `recalls=<recallCount>` since the feature shipped, but the actual promotion gate checks the *total signal count* (`recallCount + dailyCount + groundedCount`), not `recallCount` alone.

This produces confusing auditing output: entries that qualified entirely via daily-ingestion signals (`dailyCount`) show `recalls=0` in the annotation, giving the impression they bypassed the `minRecallCount` threshold when they actually satisfied it through accumulated daily signals.

Reported in #87588 — 58 of 79 promoted entries in the reporter's system have `recallCount=0` but passed via `dailyCount=5-7`.

## Fix

**`buildPromotionSection` annotation** — compute `signalCount` from the candidate's breakdown fields and emit `signals=<N>` alongside the existing `recalls=<N>` field:

```
Before: [score=0.832 recalls=0 avg=0.620 source=memory/2026-05-24-0003.md:9-10]
After:  [score=0.832 signals=6 recalls=0 avg=0.620 source=memory/2026-05-24-0003.md:9-10]
```

The `signals` value is `candidate.signalCount ?? (recallCount + dailyCount + groundedCount)`, matching exactly what `totalSignalCountForEntry` computes for the promotion gate.

**JSDoc on `minRecallCount` fields** — Clarify in `RankShortTermPromotionOptions` and `ApplyShortTermPromotionsOptions` that despite the name the parameter gates on total signal count, not `recallCount` alone. The parameter name is kept as-is for config backwards compatibility.

## Testing

- All 44 existing tests in `short-term-promotion.test.ts` pass (vitest run)
- No new TypeScript errors in the changed file
- Annotation change is additive — `recalls=` still present for anyone grepping existing logs

## Notes

The config parameter rename (`minRecallCount` → `minSignalCount`) suggested in the issue would be a breaking change for existing gateway configs. This PR takes the non-breaking approach: add JSDoc and fix the observable annotation. A separate migration/alias PR could address the rename if maintainers want to pursue it.

Closes #87588

target.title: fix(agents): match provider-scoped model ids in context-window-guard
target.body:
## Summary

`resolveContextWindowInfo` now falls back to a provider-prefix-aware match when the strict `m.id === params.modelId` comparison fails. This handles two common mismatch cases:

1. **Runtime sends scoped id, config has bare id:** runtime resolves `openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` but the user configured `id: "anthropic/claude-opus-4-6"` in `models.providers.openrouter.models[]`.
2. **Config has scoped id, runtime uses bare id:** config entry is fully-qualified but runtime passes just the model portion.

Without this fix, the configured `contextTokens` / `contextWindow` overrides are silently ignored and the system falls through to runtime model metadata or the default token count.

## Changes

- `src/agents/context-window-guard.ts`: Add fallback matching logic after the strict id check. The fallback compares trailing segments and checks prefix prepend/strip permutations.
- `src/agents/context-window-guard.test.ts`: Two regression tests covering both directions.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Fix entry.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Context window guard silently ignores user-configured `contextWindow` overrides when runtime model id uses provider-scoped format (e.g. `openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`) that doesn't match the bare config entry id (#76532).
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.2, Darwin 25.4.0 (arm64), OpenClaw 2026.4.21, node v25.5.0. Production config using OpenRouter as primary provider.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Verified the mismatch exists in live config — runtime uses `openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` while provider models are configured with shorter ids. Applied the fix and confirmed resolution:
```
node -e "
const { resolveContextWindowInfo } = require(./dist/agents/context-window-guard.js);
const result = resolveContextWindowInfo({
  modelId: openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4-6,
  models: [{ id: anthropic/claude-opus-4-6, contextWindow: 200000 }],
  providerName: openrouter
});
console.log(JSON.stringify(result));
"
```
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the above command showing the fallback match resolves correctly:
```json
{"contextWindow":200000,"source":"config-model-fallback"}
```
Live OpenClaw config demonstrating the exact mismatch pattern this fixes:
```json
"model": {
  "primary": "openrouter/anthropic/claude-opus-4-6",
  "fallbacks": ["openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6", "openrouter/x-ai/grok-4.3"]
}
```
While `models.providers.openrouter.models[]` entries use `"id": "anthropic/claude-opus-4-6"` with `"contextWindow": 200000`. Without the fix, the guard falls through to default token count; with the fix, it correctly resolves the configured 200k context window.
- **Observed result after fix:** Provider-scoped model ids now correctly match against bare config entries via the fallback path. Context window resolves to user-configured value instead of falling through to default.
- **What was not tested:** The inverse direction (bare runtime id matching scoped config id) was tested only via unit test, not in a live gateway session.

Replaces #76572 (unresolvable rebase conflicts from repo restructuring).

Fixes #76532

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; achieve parity for Claude

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK seams.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge` — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix:** Daily-driver production use (multiple Slack threads/turns per day) over the past ~2 weeks on the author's setup. Tank (a codex-harness agent) and Tabitha (claude-bridge) work side-by-side on the same gateway. Transcript files at `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/transcripts/` show the projected event stream. No fresh standalone capture for this PR; proof is the ongoing production usage.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.

target.title: Repair active npm host after plugin installs
target.body:
Summary
- Detect when a managed plugin npm install prunes dependencies from the active global-prefix OpenClaw package.
- Repair the active OpenClaw package at the same installed version before reporting plugin install success, including required dependencies and optional runtime dependencies that were present before the install.
- Roll back plugin installs if active-host repair fails, and keep npm lifecycle scripts disabled during repair.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts
- git diff --check
- Testbox-through-Crabbox: tbx_01kspspe3ktdsxrpmg7b24aqtv

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Installing an external plugin into the same managed npm prefix as a global OpenClaw package can leave the active OpenClaw package missing runtime dependencies.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox with a PR-built OpenClaw tarball installed into a throwaway global npm prefix, then the published Twilio WhatsApp plugin installed into that same managed prefix.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and packed this branch, installed the tarball globally with npm --prefix, ran openclaw plugins install npm:@srinathh/openclaw-channel-twilio-whatsapp@2.1.8 --dangerously-force-unsafe-install, verified active host dependencies, then ran openclaw doctor --fix.
Evidence after fix: The Testbox run printed json5=2.2.3, croner=10.0.1, listed twilio-whatsapp in plugins, and ended with PROOF_OK active host deps survived plugin install and doctor ran.
Observed result after fix: Testbox-through-Crabbox tbx_01kspspe3ktdsxrpmg7b24aqtv completed with exit=0. Focused local tests also passed: 2 test files, 58 tests.
What was not tested: The production gateway was not restarted or repointed to this PR. AWS Crabbox did not execute because AWS lease creation hit a security-group rule limit, so the remote proof used Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.

target.title: fix(cli): reject empty config path segments
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw config` parsed dot-notation paths with `parsePath`, which silently dropped empty/whitespace key segments (consecutive, leading, or trailing dots) instead of rejecting them. A typo'd path was normalized to a different valid key — `config set gateway..port 23456` wrote `gateway.port`, and `config get .gateway.port` returned it. On `set`/`unset` this silently mutates the wrong key.

`parseRequiredPath` only guarded the fully-empty path, and the sibling parser `parseConfigPath` (`src/config/config-paths.ts`) already rejects empty segments, so the two config-path parsers disagreed. This rejects empty key segments in `parsePath` while preserving escaped dots (`a\.b`) and bracket index segments after a dot (`foo[0].bar`).

Fixes #87564

## Changes

- `src/cli/config-cli.ts`: `parsePath` now throws `Invalid path (empty segment): <raw>` for leading/trailing/double dots and whitespace-only segments. A `segmentEmitted` flag distinguishes a legitimate post-bracket boundary (`foo[0].bar`) from an empty key segment.
- `src/cli/config-cli.test.ts`: regression tests for `set`/`get`/`unset` covering double-dot, leading-dot, trailing-dot, and whitespace-only segments, asserting no read/write occurs.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/cli/config-cli.test.ts` — 103 passed (incl. 4 new).
- `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck core + core tests pass; conflict-marker/changelog/dependency guards pass.
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --check` clean on both files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw config set/get/unset` rejects malformed dot paths with empty segments instead of silently operating on a different key.
Real environment tested: real CLI on macOS (`pnpm openclaw ...`) against a throwaway `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, OpenClaw 2026.5.28.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$(mktemp -d)/openclaw.json
pnpm openclaw config set "gateway..port" 23456 ; echo "exit=$?"
cat "$OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH"
pnpm openclaw config get ".gateway.port" ; echo "exit=$?"
pnpm openclaw config set "gateway.port" 23456   # valid path still works
```
Evidence after fix:
```
$ openclaw config set "gateway..port" 23456
Error: Invalid path (empty segment): gateway..port      (exit 1)
$ cat "$OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH"
(no config file written)
$ openclaw config get ".gateway.port"
Error: Invalid path (empty segment): .gateway.port       (exit 1)
$ openclaw config set "gateway.port" 23456
Updated gateway.port. Restart the gateway to apply.      -> {"gateway":{"port":23456}}
```
Observed result after fix: malformed paths fail closed with exit 1 and write nothing; valid paths and bracket/escape paths are unaffected.
What was not tested: no gateway/Control-UI/runtime paths involved; change is confined to CLI config-path parsing.

target.title: Recover message-tool-only private final replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Recover `message_tool_only` turns where the assistant wrote the source reply only as private final text.
- Retry once with an explicit `message(action="send")` steer when the current source reply is missing.
- If the retry still does not produce current-source delivery evidence, return a visible runtime error payload instead of leaving the source chat silent.
- Preserve existing planning-only retry behavior and deliberate `NO_REPLY` silence.

## Problem

In `message_tool_only` delivery mode, private final text is not delivered to the source conversation. Before this change, an agent could produce the user-facing answer as private final text and the source channel would appear stuck.

The fix must not treat unrelated message sends as success. A message sent to another explicit target or session does not prove the current source conversation was answered.

## Fix

- Add a `message_tool_only` missing-source-reply recovery path in `runEmbeddedAgent`.
- Require current-source reply evidence via `messagingToolSourceReplyPayloads` before suppressing recovery.
- Add one replay-safe retry with `MESSAGE_TOOL_ONLY_RETRY_INSTRUCTION`.
- Add a source-suppressed visible error payload when retry is unavailable or exhausted.
- Clarify the system prompt so final-only answers are explicitly described as private in `message_tool_only` mode.

## Guardrails

- Explicit-route or cross-session `message` sends do not count as current-source replies.
- Planning-only turns still take the existing planning retry path before message-tool-only recovery.
- `NO_REPLY` remains silent and does not trigger retry/error recovery.
- Private final text is not republished as a source reply; the agent must use the message tool.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A `message_tool_only` source chat must not stay silent when the assistant only writes private final text. The runtime retries once with a message-tool delivery instruction and then surfaces a visible error if the source reply still was not sent.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Ubuntu, using the repo's actual Node/Corepack/pnpm validation path against the runner implementation and prompt snapshot contract.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ corepack pnpm format:check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ corepack pnpm test:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 196 passed (196)

$ corepack pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
$ node scripts/check-changed.mjs -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] typecheck core
[check:changed] typecheck core tests
[check:changed] lint core
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
(exit 0)
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output above from the patched worktree. The regression tests exercise the source-channel failure mode directly: private-final-only retries, exhausted retry returns visible error, explicit-route sends do not count as source replies, planning-only retry wins first, and `NO_REPLY` stays silent.

**Observed result after fix:** The patched runner no longer treats broad message-tool delivery evidence as proof of source delivery, preserves existing silent/planning paths, and passes the targeted runner tests plus full changed-file validation.

**What was not tested:** Native Telegram Desktop visual proof was not captured in this PR body. The repository's Mantis Telegram proof job attempted that path separately but failed before Desktop start because the Crabbox/AWS capture environment could not launch, not because this patch's runner checks failed.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `CI=1 corepack pnpm check:changed -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

## Replaces

Replaces #87455 with a smaller branch focused on the original runtime failure, while keeping the relevant guard coverage that ClawSweeper flagged on the first version of this replacement PR.
